title: GTM Regulatory Outlook — Americas — Q2 2026
summary: The United States drives 67% of Americas regulatory activity this quarter as FinCEN's fundamental BSA/AML reform closes comment June 9 and GENIUS Act implementation produces coordinated stablecoin rulemaking from FDIC, FinCEN, and NCUA through Q3. Simultaneously, DOJ expands its National Cryptocurrency Enforcement Team targeting DeFi protocols and mixer services while state-level beneficial ownership registries operationalize under the Corporate Transparency Act. title: GTM Regulatory Outlook — EMEA — Q2 2026
summary: The European Union dominates EMEA activity with 21 events as MiCA's first implementation cycle produces the critical August 31 review consultation, AMLA's Q3 operational launch, and 211 CASPs now authorized across 23 EEA countries. UK runs parallel with 6 developments including FCA's crypto authorization gateway finalization, while Middle East markets (UAE, Saudi Arabia, Israel) converge toward MiCA-aligned frameworks.
